rawText:
The peer review process, in which submitted manuscripts are evaluated by independent experts before publication, is frequently described as the gold standard of academic quality assurance — yet the confidence this metaphor inspires obscures a more complicated reality in which the system's strengths and its vulnerabilities are deeply intertwined.

At its best, peer review functions as a distributed epistemic filter: multiple specialists, each bringing domain-specific expertise, scrutinize a manuscript's methodology, logic, and evidential claims, catching errors that the original authors, precisely because of their closeness to the work, may have been unable to detect.

The practice of blind review, in which the identities of the reviewers are concealed from the authors and sometimes vice versa, is intended to insulate evaluative judgments from the distorting influence of professional reputation, institutional prestige, and personal relationships — factors that, left unchecked, would reduce the assessment of ideas to the assessment of individuals.

What blind review cannot eliminate, however, is the subtler bias that operates through methodological orthodoxy: reviewers trained within a particular paradigm are more likely to approve work that conforms to that paradigm's assumptions, a tendency that can function as an invisible gatekeeping mechanism against genuinely novel approaches.

The gatekeeping function of refereed journals is thus double-edged: it protects the literature from claims that lack sufficient evidential support, but it simultaneously creates a conservative pressure that can delay the acceptance of findings that challenge established consensus, sometimes by years or even decades.

Replicability — the capacity of independent researchers to reproduce a study's results using the same methods — is a criterion that peer review notoriously struggles to verify, because reviewers evaluate manuscripts on paper rather than in the laboratory, and the incentive structures of academic publishing reward novelty far more generously than they reward confirmation.

The result is a publication landscape in which the most cited papers are often those reporting surprising or counterintuitive findings, while the methodologically rigorous replications that would confirm or disqualify those findings languish in obscurity, unfunded and frequently unpublishable.

Anonymized review, for all its virtues, also creates an accountability vacuum: a reviewer who recommends rejection need not publicly defend that recommendation, and the absence of transparency can permit evaluations that are careless, ideologically motivated, or simply uninformed to carry the same institutional weight as those that are scrupulously fair.

Proposals for open peer review — in which reviews are signed and published alongside the article — seek to address this deficit by making the evaluative process itself subject to scrutiny, though critics worry that transparency would introduce its own distortions, discouraging junior scholars from offering candid assessments of senior colleagues' work.

The scholarly consensus that emerges from peer-reviewed literature is, therefore, not the unmediated voice of nature speaking through data, but the product of a human institution with identifiable incentive structures, blind spots, and corrective mechanisms — a fact that, far from undermining trust in science, should deepen our understanding of what scientific knowledge actually is.

overview.title: How Peer Review Shapes What We Know

sentences.text: Before a science paper is published, other scientists read it carefully to check if the work is good — this is called peer review.
sentences.translation: 과학 논문이 출판되기 전에 다른 과학자들이 그 연구가 좋은지 주의 깊게 읽어보는데, 이것을 동료 심사라고 해요.

sentences.text: Sometimes the reviewers do not know who wrote the paper, which helps them judge the ideas fairly without being influenced by who the author is.
sentences.translation: 때로는 심사자가 누가 논문을 썼는지 모르는데, 이것이 저자가 누구인지에 영향받지 않고 아이디어를 공정하게 판단하는 데 도움이 돼요.

sentences.text: But peer review is not perfect — reviewers might reject new ideas just because those ideas are different from what everyone already believes.
sentences.translation: 하지만 동료 심사가 완벽하지는 않아요 — 심사자들이 새로운 아이디어를 모든 사람이 이미 믿는 것과 다르다는 이유만으로 거절할 수도 있어요.

sentences.text: One big problem is that it is hard for reviewers to check if an experiment can be done again with the same results, because they only read about it on paper.
sentences.translation: 큰 문제 중 하나는 심사자가 실험을 같은 결과로 다시 할 수 있는지 확인하기 어렵다는 것인데, 종이 위에서만 읽기 때문이에요.

sentences.text: Some people think reviews should not be secret and should be published so that everyone can see if the review was fair.
sentences.translation: 어떤 사람들은 심사가 비밀이 아니어야 하고 심사가 공정했는지 모두가 볼 수 있도록 출판되어야 한다고 생각해요.

sentences.text: Even though peer review has problems, understanding how it works helps us be smarter about what we read in science.
sentences.translation: 동료 심사에 문제가 있더라도 어떻게 작동하는지 이해하면 과학에서 읽는 것에 대해 더 똑똑해지는 데 도움이 돼요.

sentences.text: The article examines peer review as academic publishing's central quality control mechanism, arguing that the process is both indispensable and systematically flawed.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 동료 심사를 학술 출판의 핵심 품질 관리 메커니즘으로 검토하며, 이 과정이 불가결하면서도 체계적으로 결함이 있다고 주장해요.

sentences.text: Blind review aims to separate ideas from identities, but it cannot prevent the subtler bias of methodological orthodoxy, where reviewers favor work that fits their own paradigm.
sentences.translation: 블라인드 심사는 아이디어를 정체성으로부터 분리하는 것을 목표로 하지만, 심사자가 자신의 패러다임에 맞는 연구를 선호하는 방법론적 정통주의의 더 미묘한 편향은 막을 수 없어요.

sentences.text: The gatekeeping function is described as double-edged: protecting against weak claims while potentially blocking genuinely innovative research for years.
sentences.translation: 문지기 기능은 양날의 검으로 묘사돼요: 약한 주장을 막으면서도 잠재적으로 진정으로 혁신적인 연구를 몇 년간 차단할 수 있어요.

sentences.text: Replicability is identified as peer review's blind spot, since reviewers cannot verify whether results are reproducible by reading a manuscript alone.
sentences.translation: 재현성은 동료 심사의 사각지대로 식별되는데, 심사자가 원고만 읽어서는 결과가 재현 가능한지 확인할 수 없기 때문이에요.

sentences.text: The article discusses the tension between anonymized review, which protects against personal bias, and the accountability deficit it creates by shielding reviewers from public scrutiny.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 개인적 편향을 막는 익명 심사와 심사자를 공적 검증으로부터 보호함으로써 만드는 책임 결핍 사이의 긴장을 논의해요.

sentences.text: The conclusion reframes scholarly consensus not as pure truth but as the product of a human institution with incentive structures and corrective mechanisms.
sentences.translation: 결론은 학술적 합의를 순수한 진리가 아니라 인센티브 구조와 교정 메커니즘을 가진 인간 제도의 산물로 재구성해요.

sentences.text: This essay performs a structural analysis of peer review as an epistemic institution, diagnosing the tension between its filtering function (catching errors) and its gatekeeping function (enforcing orthodoxy) as constitutive rather than accidental.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 동료 심사를 인식론적 제도로서 구조적으로 분석하며, 여과 기능(오류 포착)과 문지기 기능(정통성 강제) 사이의 긴장을 우발적이 아닌 구성적인 것으로 진단해요.

sentences.text: The "gold standard" metaphor in the opening sentence is strategically deployed and immediately subverted: the essay spends ten sentences demonstrating that the standard is, in fact, alloyed with institutional impurities.
sentences.translation: 첫 문장의 "금본위" 비유는 전략적으로 배치되고 즉시 전복돼요: 에세이는 10개 문장에 걸쳐 그 기준이 실제로 제도적 불순물과 합금되어 있음을 논증해요.

sentences.text: The double-edged metaphor for gatekeeping enacts a structural ambivalence that the text refuses to resolve, positioning the reader to hold two seemingly contradictory evaluations simultaneously.
sentences.translation: "양날의 검" 비유는 텍스트가 해결하기를 거부하는 구조적 양가성을 실행하며, 독자가 겉보기에 모순되는 두 평가를 동시에 유지하도록 위치시켜요.

sentences.text: The replicability paragraph reveals the deepest structural irony: the system designed to validate knowledge cannot itself validate the most fundamental criterion of scientific validity — reproducibility.
sentences.translation: 재현성 단락은 가장 깊은 구조적 아이러니를 드러내요: 지식을 검증하도록 설계된 시스템이 과학적 타당성의 가장 근본적 기준 — 재현성 — 을 스스로 검증할 수 없다는 것이에요.

sentences.text: The concluding sentence — "not the unmediated voice of nature speaking through data, but the product of a human institution" — performs a deflationary epistemology that paradoxically strengthens rather than weakens the authority of science by making its mechanisms legible.
sentences.translation: 결론 문장 — "데이터를 통해 말하는 자연의 비매개적 목소리가 아니라 인간 제도의 산물" — 은 과학의 메커니즘을 가독적으로 만들어 역설적으로 과학의 권위를 약화시키기보다 강화하는 디플레이션적 인식론을 수행해요.

sentences.text: By ending with a claim about what scientific knowledge "actually is," the text adopts a meta-epistemic posture, treating not just specific findings but the entire knowledge-production apparatus as its object of analysis.
sentences.translation: 과학적 지식이 "실제로 무엇인지"에 대한 주장으로 끝남으로써, 텍스트는 특정 발견뿐 아니라 전체 지식 생산 장치를 분석 대상으로 다루는 메타인식론적 자세를 취해요.

sentences.original: The peer review process, in which submitted manuscripts are evaluated by independent experts before publication, is frequently described as the gold standard of academic quality assurance — yet the confidence this metaphor inspires obscures a more complicated reality in which the system's strengths and its vulnerabilities are deeply intertwined.
sentences.translation: 제출된 원고가 출판 전에 독립적인 전문가에 의해 평가되는 동료 심사 과정은 학술적 품질 보증의 금본위제로 자주 묘사된다 — 그러나 이 비유가 불러일으키는 확신은 시스템의 강점과 취약점이 깊이 얽혀 있는 더 복잡한 현실을 가린다.

grammarElements.text: The peer review process
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: in which submitted manuscripts are evaluated by independent experts before publication
grammarElements.label: in which 비제한적 관계절 (수동태)

grammarElements.text: is frequently described as the gold standard of academic quality assurance
grammarElements.label: 동사 (수동 + as 보어)

grammarElements.text: yet the confidence this metaphor inspires
grammarElements.label: yet 대조절 (목적격 관계대명사 생략)

grammarElements.text: obscures a more complicated reality in which the system's strengths and its vulnerabilities are deeply intertwined
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어 (in which 관계절)

vocabulary.example: Peer review is the primary quality control mechanism in academic publishing.
Submitted manuscripts undergo evaluation before acceptance.
The strengths and vulnerabilities of peer review are deeply intertwined.

expressions.usage: 어떤 분야에서 가장 신뢰할 수 있는 기준을 비유적으로 표현. 금본위제에서 유래한 평가적 비유.

expressions.phrase: the confidence this metaphor inspires obscures
expressions.usage: "inspires"와 "obscures" 사이의 긴장이 비유의 이중성을 드러내는 비판적 전환 표현

patterns.pattern: [Process], in which [definition], is frequently described as [positive metaphor] — yet [the confidence/trust this inspires] obscures [more complicated reality]
patterns.explanation: 비제한적 관계절로 과정을 정의하고, 긍정적 비유를 제시한 뒤, "yet"으로 그 비유의 한계를 즉시 드러내는 비판적 도입 패턴.
examples: Democracy, in which citizens select their representatives through periodic elections, is frequently described as the gold standard of political legitimacy — yet the reverence this framing commands obscures a more complicated reality in which representation and participation are often in tension.

Clinical trials, in which interventions are tested against placebos under controlled conditions, are frequently described as the gold standard of medical evidence — yet the authority this label confers obscures a more complicated reality in which design choices and funding sources shape outcomes in subtle ways.

drillQuestions.question: "Gold standard" is used here as a _____ for the highest quality benchmark.
"Yet" signals a _____ between the metaphor and reality.
"Intertwined" means the strengths and weaknesses are _____ together.
"Obscures" means the metaphor _____ the complicated reality.

sentences.notes: 글의 도입부로, "gold standard" 비유를 전략적으로 배치한 뒤 "yet"으로 즉시 전복시킨다. "in which" 관계절이 동료 심사를 정의하고, 대시 뒤의 "yet"이 비유가 가리는 복잡한 현실을 드러내는 이중 구조를 형성한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The peer review process, in which submitted manuscripts are evaluated by independent experts before publication
koreanAlignment.ko: 제출된 원고가 출판 전에 독립적인 전문가에 의해 평가되는 동료 심사 과정은

koreanAlignment.en: is frequently described as the gold standard of academic quality assurance
koreanAlignment.ko: 학술적 품질 보증의 금본위제로 자주 묘사된다

koreanAlignment.en: yet the confidence this metaphor inspires
koreanAlignment.ko: 그러나 이 비유가 불러일으키는 확신은

koreanAlignment.en: obscures a more complicated reality in which the system's strengths and its vulnerabilities are deeply intertwined
koreanAlignment.ko: 시스템의 강점과 취약점이 깊이 얽혀 있는 더 복잡한 현실을 가린다

pronunciationNotes: manuscripts: /ˈmænjəˌskrɪpts/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "매뉴스크립츠"에 가깝다.
intertwined: /ˌɪntərˈtwaɪnd/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "인터트와인드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: At its best, peer review functions as a distributed epistemic filter: multiple specialists, each bringing domain-specific expertise, scrutinize a manuscript's methodology, logic, and evidential claims, catching errors that the original authors, precisely because of their closeness to the work, may have been unable to detect.
sentences.translation: 최선의 상태에서, 동료 심사는 분산된 인식론적 필터로 기능한다: 각각 분야별 전문지식을 가져오는 복수의 전문가가 원고의 방법론, 논리, 증거적 주장을 면밀히 조사하여, 원래 저자들이 연구에 대한 근접성 때문에 감지할 수 없었을 수 있는 오류를 포착한다.

grammarElements.text: At its best
grammarElements.label: 부사구 (최선의 조건)

grammarElements.text: peer review
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: functions as a distributed epistemic filter
grammarElements.label: 동사 + as 보어

grammarElements.text: multiple specialists, each bringing domain-specific expertise, scrutinize
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 주어 (동격 분사구) + 동사

grammarElements.text: catching errors that the original authors, precisely because of their closeness to the work, may have been unable to detect
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (관계절 + 삽입 이유구 + may have been)

vocabulary.example: Reviewers scrutinize every aspect of a manuscript's methodology.
Each reviewer brings domain-specific expertise to the evaluation.
Peer review serves as an epistemic filter for academic knowledge.

expressions.usage: "at its best"가 이상적 조건을 한정하여, 현실에서는 항상 그렇지 않음을 암시하는 완화적 도입

expressions.phrase: precisely because of their closeness to the work
expressions.usage: "precisely"가 역설적 인과 관계를 강조 — 연구에 가까울수록 오류를 놓칠 가능성이 높음

patterns.pattern: At its best, [system] functions as [metaphorical role]: [agents], each [contributing specialization], [action], catching [problems] that [original agents], precisely because of [their proximity], may have been unable to [detect]
patterns.explanation: "At its best"로 이상적 조건을 한정하고, 콜론 뒤 구체적 메커니즘을 밝힌 뒤, 분사구문의 역설적 인과("precisely because of")로 시스템의 존재 이유를 설명하는 기능 분석 패턴.
examples: At its best, editorial oversight functions as a distributed quality filter: multiple editors, each bringing genre-specific judgment, scrutinize a text's structure, coherence, and factual accuracy, catching weaknesses that the original author, precisely because of their immersion in the material, may have been unable to see.

drillQuestions.question: "At its best" implies that peer review does not always function _____.
"Distributed epistemic filter" means the filtering is done by _____ people.
Authors may miss errors "precisely because of their _____" to the work.
"Scrutinize" means to examine very _____.

sentences.notes: 동료 심사의 이상적 기능을 설명하는 문장. "At its best"가 이후에 전개될 한계와 대비를 암시하고, "precisely because of their closeness"가 저자의 근접성이 역설적으로 오류 감지를 방해한다는 핵심 논리를 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: At its best, peer review functions as a distributed epistemic filter
koreanAlignment.ko: 최선의 상태에서, 동료 심사는 분산된 인식론적 필터로 기능한다

koreanAlignment.en: multiple specialists, each bringing domain-specific expertise
koreanAlignment.ko: 각각 분야별 전문지식을 가져오는 복수의 전문가가

koreanAlignment.en: scrutinize a manuscript's methodology, logic, and evidential claims
koreanAlignment.ko: 원고의 방법론, 논리, 증거적 주장을 면밀히 조사하여

koreanAlignment.en: catching errors that the original authors, precisely because of their closeness to the work, may have been unable to detect
koreanAlignment.ko: 원래 저자들이 연구에 대한 근접성 때문에 감지할 수 없었을 수 있는 오류를 포착한다

pronunciationNotes: scrutinize: /ˈskruːtɪnaɪz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "스크루터나이즈"에 가깝다.
epistemic: /ˌepɪˈstemɪk/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "에피스테믹"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The practice of blind review, in which the identities of the reviewers are concealed from the authors and sometimes vice versa, is intended to insulate evaluative judgments from the distorting influence of professional reputation, institutional prestige, and personal relationships — factors that, left unchecked, would reduce the assessment of ideas to the assessment of individuals.
sentences.translation: 심사자의 정체가 저자에게 — 때로는 그 반대로도 — 감추어지는 블라인드 심사의 관행은, 전문적 평판, 기관의 명성, 개인적 관계의 왜곡적 영향으로부터 평가적 판단을 차단하려는 것이다 — 이 요인들은 견제되지 않으면 아이디어의 평가를 개인의 평가로 축소시킬 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The practice of blind review
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: in which the identities of the reviewers are concealed from the authors and sometimes vice versa
grammarElements.label: in which 비제한적 관계절 (수동태)

grammarElements.text: is intended to insulate evaluative judgments from the distorting influence of professional reputation, institutional prestige, and personal relationships
grammarElements.label: 동사 (수동 + to부정사 + from + 3항 병렬)

grammarElements.text: — factors that, left unchecked, would reduce the assessment of ideas to the assessment of individuals
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 동격구 (관계절 + 분사 삽입 + would 가정법)

vocabulary.example: Blind review insulates judgments from personal bias.
Reviewer identities are concealed from authors in blind review.
Institutional prestige can bias the evaluation of research.

expressions.usage: "is intended to"가 설계 목적을 밝히되 실제 효과에 대해서는 유보적인 학술적 표현
"left unchecked" 축약 분사구문 + 가정법 would로 가상적 위험을 제시하는 격식적 경고 표현

patterns.pattern: The practice of [mechanism], in which [how it works], is intended to insulate [target] from [distorting influences] — factors that, left unchecked, would reduce [ideal process] to [degraded process]
patterns.explanation: 관계절로 메커니즘을 정의하고, "is intended to"로 설계 목적을 밝힌 뒤, 대시 뒤 동격구로 그 목적이 없을 때의 가상적 위험을 제시하는 제도 설계 분석 패턴.
examples: The practice of judicial recusal, in which judges withdraw from cases involving personal connections, is intended to insulate legal reasoning from the distorting influence of financial interest, social affiliation, and ideological sympathy — factors that, left unchecked, would reduce the administration of justice to the exercise of power.

drillQuestions.question: "Blind review" conceals the _____ of reviewers and authors.
"Insulate from" means to _____ against distorting influences.
"Left unchecked" means if these factors are not _____.
"Vice versa" means the _____ is also sometimes true.

sentences.notes: 블라인드 심사의 구조와 목적을 분석하는 문장. "is intended to"가 설계와 현실 사이의 간극을 암시하고, "left unchecked, would reduce"가 블라인드 심사가 없을 때의 위험을 가정법으로 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The practice of blind review, in which the identities of the reviewers are concealed from the authors
koreanAlignment.ko: 심사자의 정체가 저자에게 감추어지는 블라인드 심사의 관행은

koreanAlignment.en: and sometimes vice versa
koreanAlignment.ko: 때로는 그 반대로도

koreanAlignment.en: is intended to insulate evaluative judgments from the distorting influence of professional reputation, institutional prestige, and personal relationships
koreanAlignment.ko: 전문적 평판, 기관의 명성, 개인적 관계의 왜곡적 영향으로부터 평가적 판단을 차단하려는 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: factors that, left unchecked, would reduce the assessment of ideas to the assessment of individuals
koreanAlignment.ko: 견제되지 않으면 아이디어의 평가를 개인의 평가로 축소시킬 요인들이다

pronunciationNotes: insulate: /ˈɪnsəleɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "인설레이트"에 가깝다.
prestige: /preˈstiːʒ/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "프레스티지"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: What blind review cannot eliminate, however, is the subtler bias that operates through methodological orthodoxy: reviewers trained within a particular paradigm are more likely to approve work that conforms to that paradigm's assumptions, a tendency that can function as an invisible gatekeeping mechanism against genuinely novel approaches.
sentences.translation: 그러나 블라인드 심사가 제거할 수 없는 것은 방법론적 정통주의를 통해 작동하는 더 미묘한 편향이다: 특정 패러다임 내에서 훈련된 심사자는 그 패러다임의 가정에 부합하는 연구를 승인할 가능성이 더 높으며, 이 경향은 진정으로 새로운 접근법에 대한 보이지 않는 문지기 메커니즘으로 기능할 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: What blind review cannot eliminate
grammarElements.label: 주어 (what 명사절)

grammarElements.text: however
grammarElements.label: 삽입 접속부사 (전환)

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: the subtler bias that operates through methodological orthodoxy
grammarElements.label: 보어 (관계절)

grammarElements.text: reviewers trained within a particular paradigm are more likely to approve work that conforms to that paradigm's assumptions
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절 (과거분사 수식 + 관계절)

grammarElements.text: a tendency that can function as an invisible gatekeeping mechanism against genuinely novel approaches
grammarElements.label: 동격구 (관계절)

vocabulary.example: Methodological orthodoxy can blind reviewers to innovative approaches.
Reviewers trained within a paradigm favor conforming work.
Methodological bias can serve as an invisible gatekeeping mechanism.

expressions.meaning: 블라인드 심사가 제거할 수 없는 것
expressions.usage: what 명사절 주어가 시스템의 한계를 지적하는 비판적 전환 표현. 이전 문장의 긍정적 분석에 대한 반박 도입.
"invisible"이 편향의 비가시성을 강조하고, "against"가 배제의 방향을 명시하는 비판적 명명

patterns.pattern: What [system] cannot eliminate, however, is [subtler problem] that operates through [hidden channel]: [agents trained within X] are more likely to [favor conformity], a tendency that can function as [invisible mechanism against innovation]
patterns.explanation: what 명사절로 시스템의 구조적 한계를 지적하고, 콜론 뒤 구체적 메커니즘을 밝힌 뒤, 동격구로 그 경향을 비판적으로 명명하는 제도 비판 패턴.
examples: What diversity training cannot eliminate, however, is the subtler bias that operates through organizational culture: employees socialized within a particular corporate ethos are more likely to value behavioral conformity over genuine difference, a tendency that can function as an invisible exclusion mechanism against authentically diverse perspectives.

drillQuestions.question: "Methodological orthodoxy" means adherence to _____ methods.
"Paradigm" refers to a shared framework of _____ and methods.
"Gatekeeping mechanism" means a system that _____ access.
The bias is called "invisible" because it operates _____.

sentences.notes: 블라인드 심사의 구조적 한계를 밝히는 핵심 반박 문장. "methodological orthodoxy"라는 개념이 편향이 개인적이 아닌 구조적임을 보여주고, "invisible gatekeeping"이 이 편향의 비가시성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: What blind review cannot eliminate, however
koreanAlignment.ko: 그러나 블라인드 심사가 제거할 수 없는 것은

koreanAlignment.en: is the subtler bias that operates through methodological orthodoxy
koreanAlignment.ko: 방법론적 정통주의를 통해 작동하는 더 미묘한 편향이다

koreanAlignment.en: reviewers trained within a particular paradigm are more likely to approve work that conforms to that paradigm's assumptions
koreanAlignment.ko: 특정 패러다임 내에서 훈련된 심사자는 그 패러다임의 가정에 부합하는 연구를 승인할 가능성이 더 높으며

koreanAlignment.en: a tendency that can function as an invisible gatekeeping mechanism against genuinely novel approaches
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 경향은 진정으로 새로운 접근법에 대한 보이지 않는 문지기 메커니즘으로 기능할 수 있다

pronunciationNotes: orthodoxy: /ˈɔːrθədɑːksi/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "오서독시"에 가깝다.
paradigm: /ˈpærədaɪm/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "패러다임"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The gatekeeping function of refereed journals is thus double-edged: it protects the literature from claims that lack sufficient evidential support, but it simultaneously creates a conservative pressure that can delay the acceptance of findings that challenge established consensus, sometimes by years or even decades.
sentences.translation: 따라서 레퍼리 저널의 문지기 기능은 양날의 검이다: 충분한 증거적 뒷받침이 부족한 주장으로부터 문헌을 보호하지만, 동시에 확립된 합의에 도전하는 발견의 수용을 — 때로는 수년 또는 수십 년까지 — 지연시킬 수 있는 보수적 압력을 만든다.

grammarElements.text: The gatekeeping function of refereed journals
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is thus double-edged
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어 (thus 결론)

grammarElements.text: it protects the literature from claims that lack sufficient evidential support
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 첫째절 (관계절)

grammarElements.text: but it simultaneously creates a conservative pressure that can delay the acceptance of findings that challenge established consensus
grammarElements.label: but 대조절 (이중 관계절)

grammarElements.text: sometimes by years or even decades
grammarElements.label: 부사구 (정도 표현)

vocabulary.example: Refereed journals require peer review before publication.
Gatekeeping is double-edged: it protects but also restricts.
Findings that challenge established consensus face resistance.

expressions.usage: "thus"가 이전 분석에서 논리적 귀결을 도출하고, "double-edged"가 양면적 평가를 명시하는 분석적 결론 표현

expressions.phrase: sometimes by years or even decades
expressions.usage: "or even"이 지연의 심각성을 확대하는 강조 표현

patterns.pattern: The [function] of [institution] is thus double-edged: it [positive effect], but it simultaneously [negative effect], sometimes [extreme degree]
patterns.explanation: "thus"로 분석적 결론을 도출하고, 콜론 뒤 "it ... but it simultaneously"로 양면적 효과를 대비한 뒤, "sometimes"로 부정적 효과의 극단적 정도를 제시하는 양가적 평가 패턴.
examples: The regulatory function of licensing bodies is thus double-edged: it protects the public from unqualified practitioners, but it simultaneously creates barriers to entry that can exclude talented individuals from underrepresented backgrounds, sometimes for an entire career.

drillQuestions.question: "Refereed journals" are journals that use _____ review.
"Double-edged" means having both _____ and negative effects.
The conservative pressure can delay acceptance by _____ or decades.
"Established consensus" refers to what most experts currently _____.

sentences.notes: "double-edged"라는 핵심 비유가 동료 심사의 양면성을 정식화하는 문장. 콜론 뒤 보호 기능과 보수적 압력의 대비가 핵심이며, "sometimes by years or even decades"가 보수적 압력의 구체적 심각성을 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: The gatekeeping function of refereed journals is thus double-edged
koreanAlignment.ko: 따라서 레퍼리 저널의 문지기 기능은 양날의 검이다

koreanAlignment.en: it protects the literature from claims that lack sufficient evidential support
koreanAlignment.ko: 충분한 증거적 뒷받침이 부족한 주장으로부터 문헌을 보호하지만

koreanAlignment.en: but it simultaneously creates a conservative pressure
koreanAlignment.ko: 동시에 보수적 압력을 만든다

koreanAlignment.en: that can delay the acceptance of findings that challenge established consensus
koreanAlignment.ko: 확립된 합의에 도전하는 발견의 수용을 지연시킬 수 있는

koreanAlignment.en: sometimes by years or even decades
koreanAlignment.ko: 때로는 수년 또는 수십 년까지

pronunciationNotes: refereed: /ˌrefəˈriːd/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "레퍼리드"에 가깝다.
consensus: /kənˈsensəs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨센서스"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Replicability — the capacity of independent researchers to reproduce a study's results using the same methods — is a criterion that peer review notoriously struggles to verify, because reviewers evaluate manuscripts on paper rather than in the laboratory, and the incentive structures of academic publishing reward novelty far more generously than they reward confirmation.
sentences.translation: 재현성 — 독립적인 연구자가 동일한 방법을 사용하여 연구의 결과를 재현할 수 있는 능력 — 은 동료 심사가 검증하기 어렵기로 악명 높은 기준인데, 심사자가 실험실이 아닌 종이 위에서 원고를 평가하고, 학술 출판의 인센티브 구조가 확인보다 참신성에 훨씬 더 후하게 보상하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: Replicability
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: — the capacity of independent researchers to reproduce a study's results using the same methods —
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격구 (정의)

grammarElements.text: is a criterion that peer review notoriously struggles to verify
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어 (관계절)

grammarElements.text: because reviewers evaluate manuscripts on paper rather than in the laboratory
grammarElements.label: because 이유절 (rather than 대비)

grammarElements.text: and the incentive structures of academic publishing reward novelty far more generously than they reward confirmation
grammarElements.label: and 병렬 이유절 (비교급)

vocabulary.example: Replicability is fundamental to scientific validity.
Peer review notoriously struggles to verify replicability.
Academic publishing rewards novelty over replication.

expressions.usage: "notoriously"가 이 어려움이 널리 알려진 문제임을 나타내는 비판적 부사

expressions.phrase: reward novelty far more generously than they reward confirmation
expressions.meaning: 확인보다 참신성에 훨씬 더 후하게 보상하다
expressions.usage: "far more generously"의 비교급이 인센티브 불균형의 심각성을 강조하는 구조적 비판 표현

patterns.pattern: [Key criterion] — [definition] — is a criterion that [system] notoriously struggles to [verify/achieve], because [structural limitation 1], and [structural limitation 2]
patterns.explanation: 대시 삽입으로 기준을 정의하고, "notoriously struggles"로 체계적 실패를 밝힌 뒤, because + and로 이중 구조적 원인을 제시하는 제도적 결함 분석 패턴.
examples: Long-term environmental impact — the capacity to predict how an intervention will affect ecosystems over decades — is a criterion that regulatory agencies notoriously struggle to assess, because assessors evaluate proposals through models rather than through observation, and the political cycles of democratic governance reward short-term outcomes far more reliably than they reward intergenerational foresight.

drillQuestions.question: "Replicability" means the ability to _____ a study's results.
Reviewers evaluate "on paper" rather than in the _____.
"Notoriously" means this struggle is _____ known.
Publishing rewards _____ more than confirmation.

sentences.notes: 동료 심사의 가장 깊은 구조적 결함 — 재현성을 검증할 수 없다는 점 — 을 분석하는 문장. "on paper rather than in the laboratory"가 한계의 물리적 원인을 명시하고, 인센티브 불균형이 구조적 원인을 밝힌다.

koreanAlignment.en: Replicability — the capacity of independent researchers to reproduce a study's results using the same methods
koreanAlignment.ko: 재현성 — 독립적인 연구자가 동일한 방법을 사용하여 연구의 결과를 재현할 수 있는 능력

koreanAlignment.en: is a criterion that peer review notoriously struggles to verify
koreanAlignment.ko: 동료 심사가 검증하기 어렵기로 악명 높은 기준이다

koreanAlignment.en: because reviewers evaluate manuscripts on paper rather than in the laboratory
koreanAlignment.ko: 심사자가 실험실이 아닌 종이 위에서 원고를 평가하고

koreanAlignment.en: and the incentive structures of academic publishing reward novelty far more generously than they reward confirmation
koreanAlignment.ko: 학술 출판의 인센티브 구조가 확인보다 참신성에 훨씬 더 후하게 보상하기 때문이다

pronunciationNotes: replicability: /ˌreplɪkəˈbɪləti/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "레플리커빌리티"에 가깝다.
notoriously: /noʊˈtɔːriəsli/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "노토리어슬리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The result is a publication landscape in which the most cited papers are often those reporting surprising or counterintuitive findings, while the methodologically rigorous replications that would confirm or disqualify those findings languish in obscurity, unfunded and frequently unpublishable.
sentences.translation: 결과는 가장 많이 인용되는 논문이 종종 놀랍거나 반직관적인 결과를 보고하는 논문인 반면, 그 결과를 확인하거나 자격을 박탈할 방법론적으로 엄격한 재현 연구가 무관심 속에 방치되고, 자금 지원도 받지 못하며 출판도 불가능한 경우가 많은 출판 풍경이다.

grammarElements.text: The result
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: a publication landscape
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: in which the most cited papers are often those reporting surprising or counterintuitive findings
grammarElements.label: in which 관계절 (those + 현재분사)

grammarElements.text: while the methodologically rigorous replications that would confirm or disqualify those findings languish in obscurity
grammarElements.label: while 대조절 (관계절 + would 가정법)

grammarElements.text: unfunded and frequently unpublishable
grammarElements.label: 후치 형용사 병렬 (결과 보충)

vocabulary.example: Counterintuitive findings attract more attention than expected results.
Replication studies languish in obscurity without funding.
Methodologically rigorous replications are essential but undervalued.

expressions.usage: "languish"가 서서히 쇠퇴하는 이미지를 전달하고, "in obscurity"가 학계의 무관심을 비유적으로 표현

expressions.meaning: 자금 지원도 받지 못하며 출판도 불가능한
expressions.usage: 후치 형용사 2항 병렬이 재현 연구의 이중적 불이익을 간결하게 요약하는 비판적 보충 표현

patterns.pattern: The result is a [domain] landscape in which [rewarded category] [flourishes], while [neglected category] that would [serve critical function] languish(es) in obscurity, [compound deprivation]
patterns.explanation: "in which"로 왜곡된 풍경을 기술하고, "while"로 보상받는 것과 방치되는 것을 대비한 뒤, 후치 수식어로 방치의 구체적 형태를 추가하는 체계적 불균형 비판 패턴.
examples: The result is a media landscape in which the most shared stories are often those reporting conflict or outrage, while the carefully researched investigations that would inform public understanding languish in obscurity, under-resourced and frequently unread.

drillQuestions.question: "Counterintuitive" findings are those that go against common _____.
"Languish in obscurity" means to be _____ and neglected.
"Unpublishable" means journals will not _____ the research.
The contrast is between _____ findings and rigorous replications.

sentences.notes: 출판 인센티브의 왜곡이 만드는 구체적 풍경을 묘사하는 문장. "while" 대조가 참신성 편향의 결과를 선명하게 보여주며, "languish in obscurity, unfunded and frequently unpublishable"이 재현 연구의 체계적 소외를 감정적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The result is a publication landscape in which
koreanAlignment.ko: 결과는 ~인 출판 풍경이다

koreanAlignment.en: the most cited papers are often those reporting surprising or counterintuitive findings
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 많이 인용되는 논문이 종종 놀랍거나 반직관적인 결과를 보고하는 논문인 반면

koreanAlignment.en: while the methodologically rigorous replications that would confirm or disqualify those findings
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 결과를 확인하거나 자격을 박탈할 방법론적으로 엄격한 재현 연구가

koreanAlignment.en: languish in obscurity, unfunded and frequently unpublishable
koreanAlignment.ko: 무관심 속에 방치되고, 자금 지원도 받지 못하며 출판도 불가능하다

pronunciationNotes: counterintuitive: /ˌkaʊntərɪnˈtuːɪtɪv/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "카운터인투이티브"에 가깝다.
languish: /ˈlæŋɡwɪʃ/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "랭귀시"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Anonymized review, for all its virtues, also creates an accountability vacuum: a reviewer who recommends rejection need not publicly defend that recommendation, and the absence of transparency can permit evaluations that are careless, ideologically motivated, or simply uninformed to carry the same institutional weight as those that are scrupulously fair.
sentences.translation: 익명 심사는, 그 모든 미덕에도 불구하고, 책임 공백도 만든다: 거절을 권고하는 심사자는 그 권고를 공개적으로 방어할 필요가 없으며, 투명성의 부재는 부주의하거나, 이념적으로 동기 부여되거나, 단순히 무지한 평가가 꼼꼼하게 공정한 평가와 동일한 제도적 비중을 갖도록 허용할 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: Anonymized review
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: for all its virtues
grammarElements.label: 삽입 양보구 ("모든 미덕에도 불구하고")

grammarElements.text: also creates an accountability vacuum
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: a reviewer who recommends rejection need not publicly defend that recommendation
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절 (need not)

grammarElements.text: and the absence of transparency can permit evaluations that are careless, ideologically motivated, or simply uninformed to carry the same institutional weight as those that are scrupulously fair
grammarElements.label: and 병렬절 (permit + to + as 비교)

vocabulary.example: Anonymized review protects reviewers but reduces accountability.
Anonymous review creates an accountability vacuum.
Scrupulously fair evaluations deserve more weight than careless ones.

expressions.usage: "for all"이 양보를 표현하는 격식적 관용 표현. 긍정적 측면을 인정하면서 비판으로 전환.
"vacuum"이 책임이 완전히 부재하는 상태를 물리적 비유로 강조하는 비판적 명명

patterns.pattern: [System], for all its virtues, also creates [accountability/transparency deficit]: [agent] need not [face consequences], and [absence of safeguard] can permit [poor quality work] to carry the same [institutional authority] as [high quality work]
patterns.explanation: "for all its virtues"로 양보한 뒤, 콜론으로 구체적 문제를 밝히고, "can permit A to carry the same weight as B"로 질적 차이에도 불구한 제도적 동등 취급을 비판하는 양보적 비판 패턴.
examples: Tenure protection, for all its virtues, also creates a performance vacuum: a professor who consistently neglects teaching need not publicly account for that neglect, and the absence of regular evaluation can permit instruction that is indifferent, outdated, or simply perfunctory to carry the same institutional standing as that which is genuinely transformative.

drillQuestions.question: "For all its virtues" means _____ the system has good qualities.
"Accountability vacuum" means a lack of _____.
"Need not publicly defend" means there is no _____ to explain.
"Scrupulously fair" means extremely _____ and fair.

sentences.notes: 익명 심사의 역설적 부작용 — 책임 공백 — 을 분석하는 문장. "for all its virtues"가 양보 후 비판으로 전환하고, 부주의/이념적/무지한 평가와 공정한 평가가 동일한 제도적 비중을 갖는 구조적 문제를 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: Anonymized review, for all its virtues, also creates an accountability vacuum
koreanAlignment.ko: 익명 심사는, 그 모든 미덕에도 불구하고, 책임 공백도 만든다

koreanAlignment.en: a reviewer who recommends rejection need not publicly defend that recommendation
koreanAlignment.ko: 거절을 권고하는 심사자는 그 권고를 공개적으로 방어할 필요가 없으며

koreanAlignment.en: the absence of transparency can permit evaluations that are careless, ideologically motivated, or simply uninformed
koreanAlignment.ko: 투명성의 부재는 부주의하거나, 이념적으로 동기 부여되거나, 단순히 무지한 평가가

koreanAlignment.en: to carry the same institutional weight as those that are scrupulously fair
koreanAlignment.ko: 꼼꼼하게 공정한 평가와 동일한 제도적 비중을 갖도록 허용할 수 있다

pronunciationNotes: anonymized: /əˈnɑːnɪmaɪzd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어나니마이즈드"에 가깝다.
scrupulously: /ˈskruːpjələsli/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "스크루퓰러슬리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Proposals for open peer review — in which reviews are signed and published alongside the article — seek to address this deficit by making the evaluative process itself subject to scrutiny, though critics worry that transparency would introduce its own distortions, discouraging junior scholars from offering candid assessments of senior colleagues' work.
sentences.translation: 심사가 서명되어 논문과 함께 출판되는 공개 동료 심사 제안은 평가 과정 자체를 검증의 대상으로 만들어 이 결핍을 해결하고자 하지만, 비판자들은 투명성이 그 자체의 왜곡을 도입하여 후배 학자들이 선배 동료의 연구에 대해 솔직한 평가를 제공하는 것을 억제할 것이라고 우려한다.

grammarElements.text: Proposals for open peer review
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: — in which reviews are signed and published alongside the article —
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 관계절 (정의)

grammarElements.text: seek to address this deficit by making the evaluative process itself subject to scrutiny
grammarElements.label: 동사 (seek to + by 수단)

grammarElements.text: though critics worry that transparency would introduce its own distortions
grammarElements.label: though 양보절 (that절 포함)

grammarElements.text: discouraging junior scholars from offering candid assessments of senior colleagues' work
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: Open review may discourage candid assessments.
Open review makes the evaluative process subject to scrutiny.
Open peer review seeks to address the accountability deficit.

expressions.phrase: seek to address this deficit by making
expressions.usage: "seek to"가 시도와 의도를 표현하되 성공을 보장하지 않는 학술적 완화 표현
"its own"이 해결책이 새로운 문제를 만드는 역설을 강조하는 비판적 표현

patterns.pattern: Proposals for [reform] — in which [how it works] — seek to address [deficit] by [mechanism], though critics worry that [the reform] would introduce its own [new problems], [specific concern]
patterns.explanation: 대시 삽입으로 개혁안을 정의하고, "seek to address"로 목적을 밝힌 뒤, "though"로 비판적 우려를 균형 있게 제시하는 개혁 논쟁 패턴.
examples: Proposals for algorithmic transparency — in which companies disclose the decision rules of their automated systems — seek to address the accountability deficit by making the filtering process subject to public audit, though critics worry that transparency would introduce its own risks, enabling bad actors to game systems whose logic has been made visible.

drillQuestions.question: "Open peer review" means reviews are _____ and published.
"Subject to scrutiny" means open to _____ examination.
"Candid assessments" are evaluations that are _____.
Critics worry transparency would _____ junior scholars.

sentences.notes: 공개 동료 심사 제안과 그에 대한 비판을 균형 있게 제시하는 문장. "seek to address ... though critics worry"가 개혁안의 약속과 위험을 동시에 보여주며, 후배/선배 학자 간의 권력 비대칭이 구체적 우려로 제시된다.

koreanAlignment.en: Proposals for open peer review — in which reviews are signed and published alongside the article
koreanAlignment.ko: 심사가 서명되어 논문과 함께 출판되는 공개 동료 심사 제안은

koreanAlignment.en: seek to address this deficit by making the evaluative process itself subject to scrutiny
koreanAlignment.ko: 평가 과정 자체를 검증의 대상으로 만들어 이 결핍을 해결하고자 하지만

koreanAlignment.en: though critics worry that transparency would introduce its own distortions
koreanAlignment.ko: 비판자들은 투명성이 그 자체의 왜곡을 도입할 것이라고 우려한다

koreanAlignment.en: discouraging junior scholars from offering candid assessments of senior colleagues' work
koreanAlignment.ko: 후배 학자들이 선배 동료의 연구에 대해 솔직한 평가를 제공하는 것을 억제하여

pronunciationNotes: candid: /ˈkændɪd/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "캔디드"에 가깝다.
scrutiny: /ˈskruːtəni/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "스크루터니"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The scholarly consensus that emerges from peer-reviewed literature is, therefore, not the unmediated voice of nature speaking through data, but the product of a human institution with identifiable incentive structures, blind spots, and corrective mechanisms — a fact that, far from undermining trust in science, should deepen our understanding of what scientific knowledge actually is.
sentences.translation: 따라서 동료 심사 문헌에서 나타나는 학술적 합의는 데이터를 통해 말하는 자연의 비매개적 목소리가 아니라, 식별 가능한 인센티브 구조, 사각지대, 교정 메커니즘을 가진 인간 제도의 산물이다 — 이 사실은 과학에 대한 신뢰를 훼손하기는커녕, 과학적 지식이 실제로 무엇인지에 대한 우리의 이해를 심화시켜야 한다.

grammarElements.text: The scholarly consensus that emerges from peer-reviewed literature
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 수식)

grammarElements.text: is, therefore
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 삽입 접속부사 (결론)

grammarElements.text: not the unmediated voice of nature speaking through data
grammarElements.label: not 부정 보어 (현재분사 수식)

grammarElements.text: but the product of a human institution with identifiable incentive structures, blind spots, and corrective mechanisms
grammarElements.label: but 긍정 보어 (with + 3항 병렬)

grammarElements.text: — a fact that, far from undermining trust in science, should deepen our understanding of what scientific knowledge actually is
grammarElements.label: 대시 동격구 (far from + should + what절)

vocabulary.example: Scientific consensus is not the unmediated voice of nature.
Science has built-in corrective mechanisms.
Scholarly consensus emerges from the peer review process.

expressions.phrase: not the unmediated voice of nature speaking through data, but the product of a human institution
expressions.meaning: 데이터를 통해 말하는 자연의 비매개적 목소리가 아니라 인간 제도의 산물
expressions.usage: "not A but B" 구문이 과학적 지식에 대한 소박한 관점을 교정하는 핵심 재프레이밍 표현

expressions.phrase: far from undermining trust in science, should deepen
expressions.meaning: 과학에 대한 신뢰를 훼손하기는커녕 심화시켜야 한다
expressions.usage: "far from A, B"가 예상되는 부정적 결과를 전복하고 역설적으로 긍정적 결과를 제시하는 역전 표현

patterns.pattern: The [consensus/knowledge] that emerges from [reviewed process] is, therefore, not [naive idealization] but [realistic assessment] — a fact that, far from [expected negative], should [unexpected positive]
patterns.explanation: "not A but B"로 소박한 관점을 교정하고, 대시 뒤 "far from A, should B"로 이 교정이 부정적이 아닌 긍정적임을 역설적으로 주장하는 결론적 재프레이밍 패턴.
examples: The historical understanding that emerges from archival research is, therefore, not the transparent recovery of the past as it actually was but the product of selective evidence interpreted through contemporary frameworks — a fact that, far from invalidating historical scholarship, should deepen our appreciation of what historical knowledge demands.

drillQuestions.question: "Unmediated voice of nature" is a _____ view of science.
"Far from undermining" means the opposite of _____.
"Corrective mechanisms" are processes that _____ errors.
The conclusion says understanding peer review should _____ trust.

sentences.notes: 글의 결론으로, 동료 심사 문헌에서 나온 학술적 합의의 본질을 재정의하는 문장. "not A but B"가 소박한 과학관을 교정하고, "far from undermining, should deepen"이 이 교정이 역설적으로 과학의 권위를 강화함을 주장한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The scholarly consensus that emerges from peer-reviewed literature is, therefore
koreanAlignment.ko: 따라서 동료 심사 문헌에서 나타나는 학술적 합의는

koreanAlignment.en: not the unmediated voice of nature speaking through data
koreanAlignment.ko: 데이터를 통해 말하는 자연의 비매개적 목소리가 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but the product of a human institution with identifiable incentive structures, blind spots, and corrective mechanisms
koreanAlignment.ko: 식별 가능한 인센티브 구조, 사각지대, 교정 메커니즘을 가진 인간 제도의 산물이다

koreanAlignment.en: a fact that, far from undermining trust in science, should deepen our understanding of what scientific knowledge actually is
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 사실은 과학에 대한 신뢰를 훼손하기는커녕, 과학적 지식이 실제로 무엇인지에 대한 우리의 이해를 심화시켜야 한다

pronunciationNotes: unmediated: /ˌʌnˈmiːdieɪtɪd/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "언미디에이티드"에 가깝다.
scholarly: /ˈskɑːlərli/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "스칼러리"에 가깝다.
